# Tags

## About markdown versions

All pages in this area are available in clean markdown format. To access the markdown version of any page, append `/index.md` to the page URL.

Example:
- HTML: `https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/`
- Markdown: `https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/index.md`


## Tag Management


- [Adobe Target Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/adobe-target-extension/): The Adobe Target Extension lets you control your tests loaded by the Adobe Target Tag, all through Tealium iQ. This article describes how to configure the Adobe Target Extension in your Tealium iQ account.

- [Adobe Target Content Modification Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/adobe-target-content-modification-extension/): Use the Adobe Target Content Modification extension along with the Adobe Target tag or Adobe Experience Platform Web SDK tag to change the content of an HTML element.

- [Advanced JavaScript Code Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/advanced-javascript-code-extension/): Use the Advanced JavaScript Code extension to write JavaScript ES5 code in an enhanced editor with syntax checking, perform code diffs, GitHub integration, and a draft mode for a safeguarded publish workflow.

- [Browser History Change Event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/browser-history-change-event/): The browser history change event monitors the browser history API for changes in navigation for single-page applications (SPA).

- [Channels Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/channels-extension/): The Channels extension is used to get a view of your customer's journey across multiple channels.

- [China CDN Deployment Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/china-cdn-deployment-extension/): The China CDN Deployment extension is a Pre Loader extension that runs before the Data Layer Processing. This extension determines a user's location (inside or outside China), which allows web site content to be sent to the user from the appropriate CDN.

- [Click event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/click-event/): The click event sends tracking data when a visitor clicks an element on a page. You can specify a full click and release, mousedown, or mouseup trigger.

- [Content Modification Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/content-modification-extension/): This article is about the Content Modification extension and how to configure it. Use the Content Modification extension to change the content of an HTML element. This is used to show A/B content based on a condition.

- [CookieConsent v3 Loader](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/cookieconsent-v3-loader-extension/): Use the CookieConsent v3 Loader extension to load and configure the open source CookieConsent consent management tool on your site.

- [CookieConsent v3 Logging](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/cookieconsent-v3-logging-extension/): Use the CookieConsent v3 Logging extension to capture user consent decisions and send them to a configurable endpoint.

- [Crypto Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/crypto-extension/): The Crypto extension adds support for cryptographic hash functions to convert or generate data layer values.

- [Currency Converter Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/currency-converter-extension/): The Currency Converter extension coverts the value of a data layer variable in one currency into a different currency.

- [Do Not Track Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/do-not-track-extension/): The Do Not Track extension creates an easy way to detect DNT and to control your tags based on that value.

- [Domain-Based Deployment Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/domain-based-deployment-extension/): Use the Domain-Based Deployment extension to load a QA or Dev version of the Universal Tag (utag.js) based on the hostname. This is typically used for validation purposes when you have the Prod instance of utag.js loading on your QA or Dev sites.

- [E-commerce Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/e-commerce-extension/): The E-commerce extension defines a standard set of e-commerce variables such as Order Total and Product IDs that get automatically mapped them to vendor tags that support them.

- [Element visibility event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/element-visibility-event/): The element visibility event sends tracking data when a visitor sees a specific element on a page. You can specify how much of the element needs to be visible and for how long before the event listener triggers.

- [Flatten JSON Objects Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/flatten-json-objects-extension/): Use the Flatten JSON Objects extension to convert a nested data layer object into a new object with only one layer of key-value pairs.

- [Form submission event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/form-submission-event/): The form submission event sends tracking data when a visitor submits a form on a page.

- [Hash change event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/hash-change-event/): The hash event sends tracking data when the anchor of the page's URL after the hash symbol (`#`) changes.

- [Hosted Data Layer Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/hosted-data-layer-extension/): The Hosted Data Layer extension is a component of the hosted data layer feature. The extension is used to configure which variables from the on-page data layer are used to fetch hosted data layer objects.

- [HTML5 video event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/html5-video-event/): The HTML5 video event sends tracking data when a visitor interacts with an embedded HTML5 video on a page. You can specify event triggers for when the visitor plays, pauses, or reaches a specific playback milestone, or when the video is buffering.

- [JavaScript Code Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/javascript-code-extension/): The JavaScript Code extension is designed for implementing custom JavaScript ES5 code through Tealium iQ Tag Management. It comes with a built-in code editor and configuration options for when and where to run the code.

- [Join Data Values Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/join-data-values-extension/): The Join Data Values extension combines multiple text values into a single text value using a separator character called a delimiter.

- [jQuery clickHandler (jQuery v1.6 and below) Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/jquery-clickhandler-extension/): Use the jQuery clickHandler extension to trigger tracking events when elements are clicked for versions 1.6 and below of jQuery.

- [jQuery onHandler (jQuery 1.7 and up) Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/jquery-onhandler-extension/): The built-in event listener system provides a no-code solution that does not require jQuery to listen for and trigger tracking events. For more …

- [Link Tracking Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/link-tracking-extension/): The Link Tracking extension provides a general link tracking solution for your web pages.

- [Lookup Table Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/lookup-table-extension/): This article is about the Lookup Table extension and how to configure it. The Lookup Table extension is used to map the value from one data layer variable to set the value in another variable.

- [Lower-Casing Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/lower-casing-extension/): The Lower-Casing extension sets data layer values to lower-case. This is a best practice for data consistency.

- [Modal Offer Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/modal-offer-extension/): The Modal Offer extension enables the insertion of a modal overlay on any pages to alert the user of promotions, call to actions, or offers.

- [Mouseover event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/mouseover-event/): The mouseover event sends tracking data when a visitor hovers their cursor over an element on a page. You can specify how long the visitor needs to hover the cursor over that element.

- [Page Load Event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/page-load-event/): The page load event sends tracking data when a visitor loads a page.

- [Page view event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/page-view-event/): The page view event sends tracking data when a visitor views a page.

- [Pathname Tokenizer Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/pathname-tokenizer-extension/): This article describes the Pathname Tokenizer extension and provides a usage example.

- [Persist Data Value Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/persist-data-value-extension/): The Persist Data Value extension is used to set a value in a first-party cookie.

- [Previous Page Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/previous-page-extension/): The Previous Page extension is used to persist the name of the previous page for use on the subsequent page.

- [Privacy Manager Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/privacy-manager-extension/): This article describes how to add and configure the Privacy Manager extension. The Privacy Manager extension is used to display privacy options to your customers.

- [Scroll event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/scroll-event/): The scroll event sends tracking data when a visitor scrolls through a screen. You can specify whether to measure the scroll amount by pixels or screen percentage. You can also specify whether to track vertical scrolling, horizontal scrolling, or both.

- [Set Data Values Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/set-data-values-extension/): The Set Data Values extension sets the value of a data layer variable using text, another data layer variable, or JavaScript code.

- [Split Segmentation Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/split-segmentation-extension/): Use the Split Segmentation extension to randomly assign your visitors into segments. This can be used for A/B testing or sampling.

- [Tealium Events Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/tealium-events-extension/): The Tealium Events extension defines standard conditions for the most commonly tracked events.

- [Tracking Opt Out Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/tracking-opt-out-extension/): Use this extension to enable Tracking Opt Out. You will be provided an Opt Out link to put on your web site to allow customers to select the Opt Out option.

- [Usercentrics Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/usercentrics-extension/): The Tealium Usercentrics extension is used to enable third party CMP support. The extension simplifies the integration between tag management and consent management by allowing you to map data processing services to specific tags.

- [View-Through Tracking Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/extensions-list/view-through-tracking-extension/): Use the View-Through Tracking extension to configure a cross-domain view-through tracking pixel for use in your ad creative. This feature includes a complimentary tier of 120 million impressions with overage charges incurred beyond that. Contact your account manager for information about pricing and usage.

- [Vimeo event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/vimeo-event/): The Vimeo event sends tracking data when a visitor interacts with an embedded Vimeo video on a page. You can specify event triggers for when the visitor plays, pauses, or reaches a specific playback milestone, or when the video is buffering.

- [YouTube event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-types/youtube-event/): The YouTube event sends tracking data when a visitor interacts with an embedded YouTube video on a page. You can specify event triggers for when the visitor plays, pauses, or reaches a specific playback milestone, or when the video is buffering.

- [About events](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/about/): This article provides an introduction to our event system.

- [About Moments iQ](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/moments-iq/about/): This document explains Moments iQ on the Tealium platform.

- [About tags](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/about/): A tag is a term used in the web technology and analytics industry to describe a small snippet of code that is placed on a website for the purpose of …

- [Manage events](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/manage/): This article describes how to manage event listeners.

- [Event scope](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/event-scope/): This article describes the scope used for event listeners.

- [Event element selectors](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/selectors/): This article describes the element selectors used with event listeners.

- [Subscribe a tag to an event](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/subscribe-tags-to-events/subscribe/): This article explains how to subscribe a tag to an event listener.

- [Event triggers and frequency](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/triggers-and-frequency/): This article describes event triggers.

- [Event trigger variables](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/variables/): This article explains how to add or customize event trigger variables.

- [Event rules](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/rules/): This article describes event rules.

- [About hosted data layer](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/hosted-data-layer/about-hosted-data-layer/): Hosted data layer facilitates the use of statically hosted data for the purpose of supplementing the dynamic on-page data layer of your website or for …

- [About load rules](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/load-rules/about/): This article provides information on adding and managing load rules. Load rules are conditions that define when and where to load a tag.

- [About templates](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/templates/about/): This document introduces basic concepts about iQ Tag Management templates.

- [About the data layer](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/about/): Learn the basics of the data layer for Tealium iQ Tag Management.

- [About the data layer converter](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/data-layer-converter/about-data-layer-converter/): The data layer converter lets you keep a nested data layer object and convert it to the flat format expected by iQ Tag Management.

- [About the tag marketplace](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tag-marketplace/about/): The tag marketplace makes it easy to find and add a tag to your site.

- [About the Universal Tag (utag.js)](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/install/about-utag/): Learn how the Tealium Universal tag (`utag.js`) and  vendor library files are loaded in the page.

- [Tag and event load rules logic](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/subscribe-tags-to-events/tag-event-load-rules-logic/): This article explains tag and event load rules logic.

- [Publishing best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/publishing-best-practices/): This article presents a procedure for developing, testing, and publishing updates to a Tealium iQ Tag Management profile.

- [What is Tealium iQ Tag Management?](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/what-is-tealium-iq/): If you're new to Tealium and tag management, this overview will prepare you to start working with iQ Tag Management.

- [How the data layer works](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/how-it-works/): This article explains how the data layer works for websites.

- [Data layer best practices](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/best-practices/): This article reviews our best practices for implementing and managing your data layer.

- [Tag, event, and load rules examples](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/events/subscribe-tags-to-events/tag-event-load-rules-examples/): This article provides examples of how tags, event listeners, and load rules work together.

- [Use Case — AMP Analytics Remote Configuration File](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/hosted-data-layer/use-case-amp-analytisc-remote-configuration-file/): This section illustrates a use case that shows how hosted data layer can be used to serve a remote JSON configuration file for an implementation of …

- [Data layer variable types](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/data-layer-variables/): This article describes how to use the various data layer variable types available in Tealium iQ Tag Management.

- [Code Center](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/install/code-center/): The Code Center is where you get the code to add the Universal Tag (utag.js) to your website.

- [Example load rules](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/load-rules/examples/): The following examples are common load rules.

- [Manage tags](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/manage/): This article explains how to manage tags.

- [Advanced tag settings](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/advanced-settings/): The advanced settings control how the tag is loaded on the page and when it will fire.

- [Convert tracking calls](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/data-layer-converter/convert-tracking-calls/): You must convert data layer on tracking calls if you are passing your third-party data layer object to your Tealium tracking calls.
Example: …

- [Moments iQ Expertise example](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/moments-iq/example/): This article provides an example of Moments iQ experience leading a visitor through expertise assessment.

- [Manage variables](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/manage-variables/): This article explains how to manage variables.

- [Tealium iQ Dashboard](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/iq-dashboard/): The iQ Tag Management dashboard provides an overview of your account's configuration, Tealium server usage, and published changes.

- [Web install concepts](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/web-install-concepts/): Learn the basics of a web installation.

- [Data bundles](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/data-bundles/): This guide describes data bundles and how to access and import them.

- [Moments iQ Expertise with AudienceStream example](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/moments-iq/audiencestream-example/): This article provides an example of a Moments iQ experience leading a visitor through expertise assessment and enriching a visitor profile with this information.

- [Tealium Generic Tag](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tealium-generic-tag/): This article shows how to set up and configure the Tealium Generic Tag, a one-size-fits-most generic tag container used for implementing almost any third-party vendor image, iframe, or script-style tag to your pages.

- [Customize the data layer converter](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/data-layer-converter/customize-data-layer-converter/): Customize the behavior of the converter using the following utility functions:
teal.ignore_keys Specify the strings of text that, if a key in the …

- [Tag basics](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/tag-basics/): Learn the basics of the tag in iQ Tag Management.

- [Tealium Pixel Container Tag](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tealium-pixel-container-tag/): The Tealium Pixel Container is a tag that can load up to eight pixels on the same page. This tag can be used to implement vendor tags that have not yet been added to the tag marketplace.

- [Extension basics](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/extension-basics/): Learn the basics about extensions.

- [Tealium Custom Container Tag](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tealium-custom-container-tag/): The Tealium Custom Container is a tag that provides a JavaScript template with a code framework for implementing any kind of JavaScript tag.

- [Order of operations](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/order-of-operations/): This article describes the order of operations for the Universal Tag (utag.js) and how it relates to your configuration in iQ Tag Management.

- [Tag reporting](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tag-reporting/): The tag reporting view shows usage and performance data for the tags implemented on your site.

- [How Tealium files are cached](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/file-cache/): Files served from Tealium set the Cache-Control header to tell the browser how long to cache a file before checking for a newer version. When the …

- [About persistent cookie matching](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/cookie-matching/): Persistent cookie matching works with cookie matching tags in iQ Tag Management to request a third-party vendor user identifier.

- [About data mappings](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-mappings/about/): This article describes how to configure tag data mappings to send data from your data layer to a vendor.

- [About extensions](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/about/): Extensions provide a variety of ways to customize your implementation by offering a simple configuration interface to add complex logic without the need for coding.

- [About first-party domains](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/first-party-domains/about/): This article provides an overview of the first-party domains feature, the validation process for configured domains, and the requirements for domain certificates.

- [About linking to GitHub](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/linking-to-github/about/): This article describes how to create a secure link between your GitHub account and your iQ Tag Management account using an access token.

- [About profile libraries](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/profiles/profile-libraries/about/): This article describes profile libraries in iQ Tag Management.

- [About profiles](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/profiles/about/): This article describes profiles in your iQ Tag Management account.

- [Best Practices for Tealium iQ](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/best-practices/): A guide to best practices for implementing Tealium iQ.

- [Tealium Tools Browser Extension](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tealium-tools/browser-extension/): The Tealium Tools extension provides a standard set of tools used to assist in managing your Tealium deployment and a powerful framework for creating and sharing custom tools from the developer community.

- [How utag.sync.js works](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/install/utag-sync/): This article explains how to use utag.sync.js to implement A/B testing and multivariate tags.

- [Configure server-side data collection](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/first-party-domains/configure-server-side/): This article describes how to configure first-party domains for Tealium server-side data collection.

- [Configure client-side delivery](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/first-party-domains/configure-client-side/): This article describes how to configure first-party domains for Tealium iQ Tag Management client-side delivery.

- [Manage templates](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/templates/manage/): This document explains how to manage iQ Tag Management templates.

- [Detect a tag from code](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tag-marketplace/detect-a-tag-from-code/): This article explains how to detect a tag on your site from its code and add it to your Tealium profile.

- [Update endpoint configurations](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/first-party-domains/update-endpoints/): This article describes how to update your endpoint configurations after you have configured first-party domains.

- [Manage data mappings](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-mappings/manage/): This article explains how to manage data mappings.

- [Manage First-Party Domains](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/first-party-domains/manage/): This article provides information about adding domains to a certificate, removing domains, and viewing contact information for domains.

- [Tag marketplace policy](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tags/tag-marketplace/tag-marketplace-policy/): The tag marketplace policy lets you control which vendor tags are available in your tag marketplace to your users.

- [About user permissions](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/user-permissions/about/): This article describes how user permissions work in Tealium iQ Tag Management.

- [Tealium Tools Environment Switcher](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tealium-tools/environment-switcher/): This article describes the Environment Switcher, which is available as a Tealium Tools browser extension.

- [Manage extensions](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/manage/): This article explains how to manage extensions.

- [Manage linking to GitHub](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/linking-to-github/manage/): This article explains how to manage linking to GitHub.

- [Manage profiles](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/profiles/manage/): This article explains how to manage profiles.

- [Manage user permissions](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/user-permissions/manage/): This article explains how to manage user permissions.

- [Profile switcher](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/profiles/profile-switcher/): This article explains how to use the profile switcher.

- [Load Order Manager](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/extensions/load-order-manager/): Improve the way you view and manage tags and extensions with the Load Order Manager. The Load Order Manager combines tags and extensions on a single …

- [Manage profile libraries](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/profiles/profile-libraries/manage/): This article explains how to manage profile libraries.

- [Universal Tag Debugger](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tealium-tools/utag-debugger/): The Universal Tag (UTAG) Debugger is a tool used for validating the Universal Data Object and tracking calls.

- [User settings](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/user-settings/): This article explains how to access and view your user settings.

- [Validate and troubleshoot tags](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/troubleshooting/validate-tags/): This guide explains how to validate client-side tags that have been implemented in Tealium iQ. It will cover several methods to ensure that tags are loading and that data is being sent.

- [Web Companion](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tealium-tools/web-companion/): Web Companion lets you inspect and validate that tags and extensions are loading on your web pages.

- [About labels](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/labels/about/): Labels help you organize and filter your configuration.

- [Web Companion Adobe Test & Target](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tealium-tools/web-companion-tool-adobe-test-amp-target/): To use the Adobe Test &amp;amp; Target tool in Web Companion:
Click on the Web Companion bookmarklet to open Web Companion.
Navigate to the Tools tab. …

- [Manage labels](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/labels/manage/): Managing labels requires the Save Existing Versions permission. Creating, editing, and deleting labels does not require that you save the profile, but …

- [Create a mobile profile](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/profiles/creating-a-mobile-profile/): This document explains how to create a mobile profile.

- [Self-hosting utag.js](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/install/self-host/): This guide shows the configuration and workflow for self-hosting the utag.js files.

- [About saving](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/about-saving/): Learn about saving your changes and the difference between versions and overwritten versions.
Saving As you add, edit, or remove items from your …

- [About publishing](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/about-publishing/): Publishing is an optional and additional action during the save process. When you publish changes, the files associated with the account profile are …

- [Manage Environments](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/manage-environments/): The Manage Environments screen is where you get the code snippet to add the Universal Tag (utag.js) to your website or create custom publishing environments.

- [Save and publish a version](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/save-publish-a-version/): This document explains how to save and publish your settings.

- [Publish configuration](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/publish-configuration/): Use the following steps to configure your publish settings:
In the admin menu, click Configure Publish Settings. The Publish Configuration dialog …

- [Methods to switch publish environment](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/troubleshooting/switch-environoment/): This article describes the methods available to switch the publish environment of the Universal Tag (utag.js) file loaded on your site.

- [Version history](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/version-history/): On accounts with platform permissions enabled, only users with the Client-Side Versions permission can access this screen. For more information, see …

- [Custom publish environments](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/custom-publish-environments/): If you need more than the three default environments, you can create custom environments to give you more options.

- [Publishing workflow](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/publish-workflow-management/): The Workflow Management feature activates a workflow process where all publishes to the Prod require approval.

- [Version labels](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/version-labels/): Version labels help you organize your publish version history.

- [Version diff tool](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/version-diff-tool/): The diff tool is built for version control and distro verification. It lets you compare the versions published in your profile and inspect their distro files.

- [Merge versions](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/merging-versions/): The merge feature is useful when you have to bring changes from another version into the currently-published version.

- [Concurrent users (iQ Tag Management)](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/concurrent-users/): In iQ Tag Management, concurrent user functionality offers the following features:
Profile Level Concurrency
Concurrent user tracking occurs at the …

- [Merging changes between concurrent users](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/merging-changes-between-concurrent-users/): This article describes how to bring in changes from concurrent users of the same profile version.

- [Tealium Tools Version 3 Released](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/tealium-tools/tealium-tools-version-3/): Learn about the changes in Tealium Tools version 3.

- [Page performance](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/troubleshooting/page-performance/): Page performance measures how quickly a web page loads and renders in the browser. The primary purpose of page performance is to optimize the user experience.

- [Load the universal tag from a Chinese network](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/getting-started/install/load-utag-china/): This article describes how to improve performance when loading the universal tag from Chinese networks.

- [Navigation timing data](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/troubleshooting/nav-timing-data/): This article explains how to enable navigation timing data, which you can use to measure the performance of a website.

- [Subresource Integrity publishing workflow](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/save-publish/subresource-integrity/): This article explains how to implement Subresource integrity to ensure that your visitors receive valid utag.js files through the Tealium CDN.

- [Template Status Checker](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/templates/template-status-checker/): This article describes how to use the Template Status Checker to scan your templates and display version information and modification status.

- [Client-side bot filtering](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/administration/client-side-bot-filtering/): This article describes how to filter out bot traffic in client-side environments.

- [Resource locks](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/labels/resource-locks/): Resource locks are labels that you apply to restrict access to those elements.

- [Tealium cookies](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/data-layer/cookies/): This article provides an overview of the cookies used by Tealium iQ Tag Management and the various methods by which additional cookies can be created and modified.

- [Enhanced browser console debugging with Charles Proxy](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/troubleshooting/charles-proxy/): This article demonstrates how to use Charles Proxy to obtain more advanced output from utag.DB.

- [How do I log in to the Tealium platform?](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/frequently-asked-questions/faq-login/): This article describes how to log into the Tealium platform.

- [How do I remove a user?](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/frequently-asked-questions/how-do-i-remove-a-user/): This article describes how to remove a user from your account.

- [How do I reset my password?](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/frequently-asked-questions/how-do-i-reset-my-password/): This article describes how to reset your password.

- [How to add support for utag.link to a tag template](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/frequently-asked-questions/how-to-add-support-for-utaglink-to-a-tag-template/): This article describes how to add support for utag.link to a tag template.

- [How do I use an array variable in a load rule?](https://docs.tealium.com/iq-tag-management/frequently-asked-questions/how-do-i-use-an-array-variable-in-a-load-rule/): This article describes how to use an array variable in a load rule.
